evidence: "This is British humour at one of its best."
"This is a good film to watch on Valentine's Day or if you are ever in the mood for a good romantic comedy."
"The storyline is pretty standard (overweight, pessimistic girl think she can't find love - but runs into the man of her dreams)"
"The whole storyline is predictable and it's another example of Hollywood using the same formula at scoring for an audience."
"There was too much predictable shrieking, screaming, and yelling that it just got annoying after awhile."
"The movie lacks a lot of the sophistication that an adult viewer may appreciate."
"The movie is composed of eight dreams, however it flows very nicely, so there is never a sense of hesitation between each of the transitions."
"It was beautifully filmed and directed by the legendary Akira Kurosawa and probably one of his best works ever."
"I normally am a huge fan of cheezy, leg warmer, roller boogie 70's and 80's musical films, however this one unfortunately did not live up to my expectations."
"Beautifully (and amazingly!) shot, you will feel like you are flying with the birds in this film."
"The backdrop (Southern & Northern California), the wonderful musical score (Simon & Garfunkel) and great cast made this movie very worthwhile to see as well."
"This is a very powerful, moving film."
"It will not only inspire you but teach you about a culture that is not often talked about."

summary: The user shows a strong preference for comedies and romantic films, often appreciating visual quality and emotional depth. However, they tend to dislike predictable storylines and slow pacing, indicating a desire for more engaging narratives.
